evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Assess data quality, bias, and limitations; Work within data governance, privacy, and ethical frameworks; Solid understanding of data quality and assurance principles
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour: multiple governance/quality signals (data governance, privacy frameworks, data lineage, quality assurance) dominate over speed language.
evidence.domain_risk: major infrastructure programme; direct impact on daily commutes and city sustainability
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Moderate: public-facing impact on transport/city systems but not financial reporting or regulatory compliance in JD language.
evidence.data_team_maturity: greenfield opportunity; team is still maturing; help shape processes, influence how analytics is done
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Early: explicit greenfield signal, team still maturing, candidate will help establish processes and influence function direction.
evidence.jd_authorship: Build reproducible analytical workflows; Perform statistical analysis using regression, hypothesis testing, sampling; Conduct exploratory data analysis; Query, validate, and manipulate data using SQL across multiple data sources
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Hiring manager: specific technical methodologies (regression, hypothesis testing, EDA, SQL validation) and tools (pandas, numpy, Jupyter) show deep domain understanding.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: data infrastructure; data governance; reproducible analytical workflows; insights to stakeholders
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Internal data: role focuses on building analytics infrastructure and processes for the data function itself, not commercial/product/finance audiences.
evidence.autonomy_level: you'll help shape processes; influence how analytics is done; define; establish the foundation; set direction
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic: candidate sets direction and shapes processes in a greenfield function, not executing scoped work.
evidence.ai_role: Use AI tools to enhance data analysis, code generation, and insight discovery with careful validation
evidence.ai_role_explanation: AI user: candidate expected to use generative AI tools (copilots, coding agents) to accelerate their own analysis and code generation.
evidence.testing_framing: Perform statistical analysis using hypothesis testing; Build reproducible analytical workflows; Assess data quality, bias, and limitations
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Responsibility: hypothesis testing and data quality assessment framed as core responsibilities the candidate owns, not just tool familiarity.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Work within data governance, privacy, and ethical frameworks; Communicate analytical findings in a way that supports decision-making without overstating certainty; Assess data quality, bias, and limitations, and clearly articulate what the data can and cannot support
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High: governance, privacy, bias assessment, and careful communication about data limitations dominate early-career greenfield function to prevent stakeholder misuse.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: real greenfield opportunity; team is still maturing; help shape processes
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Greenfield: explicit signal that team is building analytics capability from early stage with no mature infrastructure.
evidence.loss_aversion: Work within data governance, privacy, and ethical frameworks at all times; Communicate analytical findings in a way that supports decision-making without overstating certainty.
